Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: The final detection mechanism available to aircraft is the Magnetic Anomaly Detector (MAD).

MAD is deployed from a 'MAD bird', which is streamed out aft of the helicopter. MAD works by detecting variations in the Earth's magnetic field caused by large masses of ferrous material, such as the steel hull of a submarine. Note: submarines with titanium hulls, such as the Soviet Alfa class, are very hard to detect with MAD gear!

MAD gear requires the helicopter to fly very tight passes at low altitude. You can conduct a MAD search by ordering the helicopter to its minimum altitude, after which the MAD bird will be streamed and pulled along behind the helicopter. MAD contacts will appear as 'magnetic anomaly' contacts on the Tactical Display.
